content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

November 18, 2005


Terry B. Gorton
135 Sunrise Avenue
Sedona, Arizona 86336-4132

Re:     Your Request for Advice
        Our File No. A-05-220

Dear Ms. Gorton:

        This letter is in response for advice regarding the lobbying provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

QUESTION

        Even though you do not intend to lobby in California in the future, are you
required to take the lobbyist orientation course due to your past lobbying activity?

CONCLUSION

        No. You are not required to attend the lobbyist orientation course.

FACTS

        You completed the lobbyist certification papers on February 15, 2005, and
terminated your status as a lobbyist on March 31, 2005. During that period, the required
lobbyist orientation course was not offered. You do not lobby now, nor do you intend to
lobby the state of California in the future.

ANALYSIS

        Section 86103(d)(2) provides that an individual who completes a new lobbyist
certification, but has not completed the course described in Government Code section
8956(b) within the previous 12 months, must complete the course within 12 months of

___________________________
¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

File No. A-05-220
Page No. 2

signing the new certification. The lobbyist certification is considered "conditional" until
the individual has attended the course.

You completed certification papers on February 15, 2005. Therefore, the deadline
for completion of the course is February 14, 2006. Since you terminated your status as a
lobbyist prior to that date, we have determined that you are not required to complete the
lobbyist orientation course described in section 86103. This determination is based on
the particular facts of your situation, specifically that no lobbyist orientation course was
offered prior to your termination. In addition, your lobbying activity was short in
duration, you have ceased all lobbying activity in California, and you do not intend to
engage in lobbying activity in California in the future. (Wheeler Advice Letter, No. A-
92-086 and Read Advice letter, No. A-99-163.)

We note, however, that your conditional certification is void and you would not
be able to lobby in California until the requirements of section 86103 are met.²

We have sent a copy of this letter to the Secretary of State's office to serve as
explanation for why you are not required to amend your lobbyist certification.

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: Kevin S. Moen, PhD
Political Reform Consultant II
Technical Assistance Division

KSM:rd
I:\AdviceLtrs\05-220

Cc: Chief Counsel
Senate Committee on Legislative Ethics

Chief Counsel
Assembly Legislative Ethics Committee

Political Reform Division
Secretary of State

² There is no authority under the Act to waive the requirement that a lobbyist complete ethics
training. (Chafe Advice Letter, No. A-01-153 and McCormick Advice Letter, No. A-02-013.)
